answer:
The ghazal is chapter 148 ("Na tha kuch toh Khuda tha, kuch na hota toh Khuda hota") from *Diwan-E-Ghalib* by Mirza Ghalib.[^1]

**Opening paradox** (first line): "When nothing was, then God would be; if nothing were, God still would be."[^2]

This is immediately followed in the second line by the speaker's complaint: "This state of being drowned me here; had I not been, what would there be?"[^2]

**Severed-head couplet** (second line of the second stanza): "Were it not severed, it would be laid gently, grieving, on my knee."[^3]

[^1]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/148-na-tha-kuch-toh-khuda-tha-kuch-na-hota-toh-khuda-h/translation/](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/148-na-tha-kuch-toh-khuda-tha-kuch-na-hota-toh-khuda-h/translation/).
[^2]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/148-na-tha-kuch-toh-khuda-tha-kuch-na-hota-toh-khuda-h/translation/#p-1420db).
[^3]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/148-na-tha-kuch-toh-khuda-tha-kuch-na-hota-toh-khuda-h/translation/#p-6f69a2).

args.query: When nothing was, then God would be
"had I not been, what would there be"
